rows.source_ref: abokwaik, 'ATR Break Out' (ABO Trading System), ForexFactory thread 562470 (year n.d.), https://www.forexfactory.com/thread/562470/atr-break-out
rows.concept: Momentum breakout: at each new bar place buy-stop/sell-stop pending orders an ATR-multiple away from price, so a move beyond average bar size triggers a trend-following trade managed with ATR-scaled SL/TP/trailing.
rows.eligibility_reason: Pure-MQL5 ATR straddle breakout with ATR-based SL/TP/trailing stop and risk-% sizing; deterministic; FX-majors + Gold H1 data available via .DWX; each order carries its own stop (not martingale/grid).
rows.notes: One core rule set. Entry: at start of each new bar, place pending stop orders at BreakOut_ATR_mult x ATR(period, default 50) from price (straddle both sides; optional 'Follow Last Bar Direction' filters to one side); untriggered pendings deleted and re-placed each bar until filled. Exits: SL = SL_ATR_mult, TP = TP_ATR_mult, plus ATR trailing stop. Params stated: BO mult 2-3, SL 2-4, TP 4-20, TS 6, ATR period 50; optional MACD+RSI filters (suggested by auvergnat78) to cut false signals. Variants = same core: 'default' (BO x3 + RSI on) vs abokwaik 'crazy'/aggressive (BO x2, filters off, MaxOrders 99, multiple simultaneous orders). Also run on XAUUSD M15, GBP/USD M30, USD/JPY, USD/CAD (instrument-agnostic momentum). Trading-Time window configurable (author uses European session for EURUSD). Caveats: forum-sourced (reputability); user Kubiasty reported persistent losses, author admits Gold-M15 gives many false signals in ranges and default settings 'fire mostly at red-news times' (event-vol angle worth isolating); MaxOrders up to 99 / multi-order stacking = concentration risk, implement with capped max orders. MT4 .ex4 originals only (rebuild in V5 MQL5).
